subject: history
note: SAMPLE questions demonstrating the UPSC track. Facts are correct and syllabus-relevant; source = 'Sample (UPSC pattern)'. Replace with verified real PYQs + SME solutions + 13-language translations before shipping; then remove the draft flag.

prompt.en: The Quit India Movement was launched in the year:

solution.en: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 8 August 1942 at the Bombay session of the Congress. 1930 was the Civil Disobedience/Salt Movement and 1919 the Rowlatt/Jallianwala Bagh events.

prompt.en: Who was the first Governor-General of independent India?

solution.en: Lord Mountbatten was the first Governor-General of independent India (1947–48). C. Rajagopalachari was the first and only Indian to hold the post, who succeeded him.

prompt.en: The Indian National Congress was founded in the year:

solution.en: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885, with A. O. Hume playing a key role and W. C. Bonnerjee as its first president. 1857 was the Revolt; 1905 the Partition of Bengal.

prompt.en: The Battle of Plassey, which paved the way for British rule in Bengal, was fought in:

solution.en: The Battle of Plassey (1757) was won by Robert Clive against Siraj-ud-Daulah. The Battle of Buxar followed in 1764; the Revolt of 1857 came a century later.

prompt.en: Who gave the famous call 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement?

solution.en: Mahatma Gandhi gave the 'Do or Die' call in his Quit India speech of August 1942. (Subhas Chandra Bose's slogan was 'Give me blood and I will give you freedom'.)

prompt.en: The Indus Valley (Harappan) Civilisation is especially noted for its:

solution.en: The Harappan Civilisation is famous for its well-planned towns, grid street layout, and advanced drainage/sanitation. It was a Bronze Age culture (iron was not yet in use).

prompt.en: The Gandhara school of art, known for its Greco-Roman influence on Buddhist sculpture, flourished primarily under the patronage of which dynasty?

solution.en: The Gandhara school reached its peak under the Kushana rulers, especially Kanishka, blending Hellenistic techniques with Buddhist themes.

prompt.en: Which of the following was NOT one of the four noble truths preached by Gautama Buddha?

choices.en: The world is full of suffering
Desire is the cause of suffering
Rituals and sacrifices lead to salvation

solution.en: Buddha rejected elaborate rituals and sacrifices. The Four Noble Truths concern suffering, its cause (desire), its cessation, and the path to end it.

prompt.en: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded by Harihara and Bukka, who are associated with which dynasty?

solution.en: Harihara I and Bukka I founded Vijayanagara in 1336 and belonged to the Sangama dynasty, the first of the four dynasties that ruled the empire.

prompt.en: The doctrine of 'Lapse', used to annex Indian states, is most closely associated with which Governor-General?

solution.en: Lord Dalhousie applied the Doctrine of Lapse to annex states like Satara, Jhansi and Nagpur when a ruler died without a natural heir.

prompt.en: Which Mughal emperor abolished the pilgrimage tax and the jizya, promoting a policy of religious tolerance?

solution.en: Akbar abolished the jizya (tax on non-Muslims) and the pilgrimage tax as part of his policy of sulh-i-kul (universal peace).

prompt.en: The Ryotwari system of land revenue, under which settlement was made directly with the cultivator, was introduced primarily in which region?

solution.en: The Ryotwari system, developed by Thomas Munro and Alexander Read, was introduced mainly in the Madras and Bombay Presidencies, making the ryot (cultivator) the revenue payer.

prompt.en: The 'Arya Samaj' was founded in 1875 by which social reformer?

solution.en: Swami Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875, advocating a return to the Vedas and opposing idolatry and caste rigidity.

prompt.en: Which edict-issuing ancient Indian ruler adopted 'Dhamma' as a code of moral conduct after the Kalinga war?

solution.en: Ashoka embraced Buddhism and propagated Dhamma through his rock and pillar edicts after being moved by the bloodshed of the Kalinga war.

prompt.en: The Ghadar Party, formed to overthrow British rule through armed revolt, was established in 1913 in which country?

solution.en: The Ghadar Party was founded in San Francisco, USA, in 1913 by Indian immigrants including Lala Har Dayal and Sohan Singh Bhakna.

prompt.en: Who among the following presided over the first session of the Indian National Congress in 1885?

solution.en: Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee was the president of the first Congress session held in Bombay in 1885; A. O. Hume was the general secretary.

prompt.en: The Rowlatt Act of 1919, which the British passed despite Indian opposition, is best described as a law that allowed:

choices.en: A ban on the Indian National Congress
An increase in the salt tax

solution.en: The Rowlatt Act empowered the government to imprison people without trial, provoking nationwide protest and leading to the Jallianwala Bagh tragedy.

prompt.en: The famous Iron Pillar at Mehrauli, noted for its rust-resistant composition, dates to which period?

solution.en: The Mehrauli Iron Pillar, bearing an inscription referring to a king 'Chandra', is generally attributed to the Gupta period and reflects advanced metallurgy.

prompt.en: Which Sikh Guru compiled the Adi Granth, the original version of the Sikh holy scripture?

solution.en: Guru Arjan Dev, the fifth Sikh Guru, compiled the Adi Granth in 1604 at Amritsar, incorporating hymns of earlier Gurus and various saints.

prompt.en: The 'Permanent Settlement' of 1793, which fixed land revenue in perpetuity, was introduced by which official?

solution.en: Lord Cornwallis introduced the Permanent Settlement (Zamindari system) in Bengal in 1793, fixing revenue payable by zamindars permanently.

prompt.en: Kanishka, one of the greatest Kushana rulers, is credited with convening the Fourth Buddhist Council, which was held in:

solution.en: The Fourth Buddhist Council was held in Kashmir under Kanishka's patronage, and it is associated with the formal rise of Mahayana Buddhism.

prompt.en: The 'Poona Pact' of 1932 was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and which leader concerning representation of the depressed classes?

solution.en: The Poona Pact was signed between Gandhi and B. R. Ambedkar, replacing separate electorates for the depressed classes with reserved seats within the general electorate.
